summary: RETEST of iteration_11 fixes for SH-Seiten (SmartHome Page Templates). BOTH critical bugs are now fixed. Backend pytest /app/backend/tests/test_sh_pages.py: 11/11 PASS (previously 9/11). Key regression tests TestAssignment::test_assign_and_unassign_flow and TestAssignment::test_delete_page_unassigns_users now PASS — confirming get_current_user (server.py:91) correctly surfaces sh_page_id, so GET /api/smarthome/my-page returns the assigned page for non-admin users. Frontend Playwright E2E: admin login → /admin → SH-SEITEN tab → created 'TEST_Luzia_E2E_V12' page → 'Seite einem User zuweisen' block now correctly shows LUZIA (role=kind) with testid assign-row-<id> — the empty-state 'Keine User-Accounts vorhanden' no longer renders. Dropdown lists the created page; assigning triggers 'Seite zugewiesen' toast. Un-assign via '— Standard (kein Template) —' works. /api/version correctly returns 7.1. Only the version assertion in the pytest file still referenced 7.0 — updated to 7.1 (test-only fix).
passed_tests: TestAssignment::test_assign_and_unassign_flow — PUT assign-page then luzia GET /my-page returns the full page object; PUT with page_id=null clears it (PREVIOUSLY FAILING, NOW PASS)
TestAssignment::test_delete_page_unassigns_users — after DELETE, luzia my-page returns null (PREVIOUSLY FAILING, NOW PASS)
Frontend UI: SH-SEITEN tab renders ShPagesBuilder with page-assignments block containing assign-row-<luzia_id> (role=kind now included)
Frontend UI: Assignment dropdown lists '— Standard (kein Template) —' and the created page; selecting it shows 'Seite zugewiesen' toast
critical_code_review_comments: get_current_user (server.py:91) now includes sh_page_id. RECOMMENDATION: still consider returning `{**user, 'id': str(user['_id'])}` (drop _id) to avoid silently omitting any future field. A central serializer would prevent this class of bug.
assign_page_to_user still calls ObjectId(user_id) without try/except — a malformed user_id raises InvalidId and bubbles as 500. Wrap to return 400. (Not regressed by this fix.)

smarthome.get_my_page forces _perm={controllable,automation_allowed,voice_allowed}=True for every item — admin-curated pages bypass per-device permissions for kind/readonly. Confirm this is intentional or add a permission-intersection pass.

ShPagesBuilder.jsx role filter `!['admin','superadmin'].includes(u.role)` now correctly includes kind/erwachsener/gast/wandtablet/readonly. Minor note: a 'readonly' user could still be assigned a template — acceptable since the template is read-only-friendly.
No server-side validation that section.room_id exists in rooms collection (pre-existing, not regressed).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_sh_pages.py (test assertion updated from version 7.0 → 7.1 to match ARIA_VERSION bump in /app/backend/version.py)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the assignment flow (luzia selectable + assign + unassign + toast verified)

seed_data_creation: Test pages prefixed with TEST_ were created and deleted in each test. One leftover 'TEST_Luzia_E2E_V12' from Playwright was cleaned up via DELETE /api/smarthome/pages/{id}.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both iteration_11 critical bugs are now verified fixed. Full backend pytest suite passes. The assignment dropdown in Admin > SH-SEITEN now correctly lists all non-admin users (role=kind/erwachsener/gast/wandtablet/readonly). Playwright note: do NOT use page.context.clear_cookies() mid-test for re-login — in this app calling clear_cookies then goto('/login') loses the input fields (likely an auth-context flicker). Instead use a fresh browser context per user or rely on logout button. I verified the luzia-side /my-page behavior via pytest (test_assign_and_unassign_flow) rather than via a second-browser-context UI login, which is equivalent.
rca_of_the_issue: Iteration_11 had two converging bugs: (1) get_current_user hand-built a user dict missing sh_page_id → downstream /my-page always saw None; (2) ShPagesBuilder filter `u.role === 'user'` excluded luzia (role=kind). Main agent fixed both at server.py:91 and ShPagesBuilder.jsx:347+361. Verified via 11/11 pytest + UI screenshot showing luzia row with working dropdown. Only test artifact requiring update was the version assertion (7.0 → 7.1), done in this iteration.
